question:
Assume SERVEROUTPUT is ON. Given table q19_archive with the columns item_id, archived_flag. It contains exactly three rows: two rows have archived_flag='N', and one row has archived_flag='Y'.

What will this block display?

Code:
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO q19_archive(item_id, archived_flag)
  SELECT item_id + 100, 'Y'
  FROM q19_archive
  WHERE archived_flag = 'N';

  DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE('Inserted:' || SQL%ROWCOUNT);
END;
/

Assume SERVEROUTPUT is ON. Given table q19_points with the columns player_id, points. It contains exactly one row with player_id=7 and points=15.

What will this block display?

Code:
DECLARE
  v_label VARCHAR2(10);
BEGIN
  SELECT CASE
           WHEN points >= 20 THEN 'HIGH'
           WHEN points >= 10 THEN 'MID'
           ELSE 'LOW'
         END
  INTO v_label
  FROM q19_points
  WHERE player_id = 7;

  DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE(v_label || ':' || SQL%ROWCOUNT);
END;
/

Assume SERVEROUTPUT is ON. Given table q19_rentals with the columns rental_id, returned_flag. It contains exactly three rows: two rows have returned_flag='N', and one row has returned_flag='Y'.

What will this block display?

Code:
BEGIN
  UPDATE q19_rentals
  SET returned_flag = 'Y'
  WHERE returned_flag = 'N';

  DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE(CASE
                         WHEN SQL%FOUND THEN 'YES'
                         ELSE 'NO'
                       END || ':' || SQL%ROWCOUNT);
END;
/
